entries.title: Do Not Use Disney Post-Endor Defaults

context.label: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order
context.phase: Early New Republic to New Jedi Order Threshold

content.fact: Disney sequel-era institutions and outcomes do not apply to this Legends deck unless the user explicitly stacks or declares them.
content.injection: In Legends New Republic scenes, avoid First Order, Ben Solo, Rey, Snoke, sequel Jedi temple, and Disney-era political outcomes unless the story introduces them. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

continuity.sourceBoundary: Legends post-Endor New Republic material from the immediate aftermath of Return of the Jedi through the Hand of Thrawn peace settlement, before the New Jedi Order era begins.
continuity.notes: Development deck. Keep separate from Disney canon post-Endor and sequel-era continuity.

content.fact: Clone Emperor, Mara’s past, Noghri deception, Hand of Thrawn secrets, and Solo children futures should stay gated to their windows.
content.injection: Do not leak major Legends spoilers before their active Context unless the user explicitly asks for that era.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order.

context.label: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order
context.phase: Early New Republic to New Jedi Order Threshold

content.fact: Luke, Leia, Han, Wedge, and Mon Mothma are public symbols whose private choices can create political consequences.
content.injection: In New Republic scenes, let fame and public legitimacy complicate personal action. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

context.label: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order
context.phase: Early New Republic to New Jedi Order Threshold

content.fact: New Republic scenes should include committees, provisional authority, diplomacy, intelligence gaps, and fragile legitimacy when politics are involved.
content.injection: Avoid writing the New Republic as a frictionless hero machine; add governance costs when relevant. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

context.label: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order
context.phase: Early New Republic to New Jedi Order Threshold

content.fact: Luke’s Jedi restoration is fragile and cannot solve every military, family, or political problem through Force power.
content.injection: When active in Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order, even when Jedi are present, preserve logistics, politics, trauma, and non-Force expertise. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.

context.phase: Early New Republic to New Jedi Order Threshold

content.fact: Imperial holdouts include professionals, ideologues, warlords, Intelligence veterans, dark-side loyalists, and pragmatists.
content.injection: Choose the Imperial subtype for the context instead of making every Imperial act like Thrawn, Palpatine, or a random warlord. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order.

context.label: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order
context.phase: Early New Republic to New Jedi Order Threshold

content.fact: Later NJO, Legacy of the Force, and Fate of the Jedi outcomes should not shape child-era Jacen, Jaina, Anakin, or Ben Skywalker scenes.
content.injection: Before those eras, keep future tragedies, romances, dark turns, and deaths out of active character knowledge and narration. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
